mission.title: Install and Configure Modelpack on Kubernetes
mission.description: ModelPack is an open standard for packaging, distributing, and running AI models in cloud-native environments. Installing ModelPack allows you to create standard-compliant implementations for AI/ML projects, facilitating interoperability and compatibility across different platforms.

steps.title: Clone the ModelPack repository
steps.description:
Clone the ModelPack specification repository to your local machine using the following command:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/modelpack/model-spec.git
cd model-spec
```

steps.title: Build the ModelPack Docker image
steps.description:
Build the Docker image for ModelPack using the provided Dockerfile. Ensure you have Docker installed and running:
```bash
docker build -t modelpack:1.0 .
```

steps.title: Deploy ModelPack to Kubernetes
steps.description:
Create a Kubernetes deployment using the Docker image you built. Save the following YAML to a file named `modelpack-deployment.yaml`:
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: modelpack
  namespace: modelpack
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: modelpack
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: modelpack
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: modelpack
        image: modelpack:1.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
``` 
Then apply the deployment:
```bash
kubectl apply -f modelpack-deployment.yaml --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Expose ModelPack service
steps.description:
Create a service to expose your ModelPack deployment. Save the following YAML to a file named `modelpack-service.yaml`:
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: modelpack
  namespace: modelpack
spec:
  type: NodePort
  ports:
  - port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
  selector:
    app: modelpack
```
Then apply the service:
```bash
kubectl apply -f modelpack-service.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the ModelPack pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n modelpack
```
You can also port-forward to access the service locally:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/modelpack 8080:8080 -n modelpack
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the ModelPack deployment. Edit the `modelpack-deployment.yaml` file to include resource limits:
```yaml
resources:
  requests:
    memory: "256Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
  limits:
    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "1"
```
Then reapply the deployment:
```bash
kubectl apply -f modelpack-deployment.yaml -n modelpack
```

uninstall.title: Remove the ModelPack service
uninstall.description:
Delete the service you created:
```bash
kubectl delete -f modelpack-service.yaml -n modelpack
```

uninstall.title: Remove the ModelPack deployment
uninstall.description:
Delete the deployment:
```bash
kubectl delete -f modelpack-deployment.yaml -n modelpack
```

uninstall.title: Clean up the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace created for ModelPack:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace modelpack
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current deployment configuration to a YAML file:
```bash
kubectl get deployment modelpack -n modelpack -o yaml > modelpack-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Docker image
upgrade.description:
Make any necessary changes to the code or Dockerfile, then rebuild the Docker image:
```bash
docker build -t modelpack:1.1 .
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the deployment
upgrade.description:
Update the deployment to use the new image version:
```bash
kubectl set image deployment/modelpack modelpack=modelpack:1.1 -n modelpack
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the ModelPack pods are running with the new image:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n modelpack
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are in a 'Pending' state, check the events for the namespace:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n modelpack
```
Ensure that there are sufficient resources available in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the service, check the service configuration:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n modelpack
```
Ensure that the service type is set to NodePort or LoadBalancer and that the correct ports are exposed.

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are in a 'CrashLoopBackOff' state, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n modelpack
```
Investigate the logs for any application errors or misconfigurations.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of ModelPack will have the deployment running with the specified number of replicas, and the service will be accessible through the defined port. You can verify the installation by checking the pod status and accessing the service via port-forwarding.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: modelpack
  namespace: modelpack
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: modelpack
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: modelpack
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: modelpack
        image: modelpack:1.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: modelpack
  namespace: modelpack
spec:
  type: NodePort
  ports:
  - port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
  selector:
    app: modelpack

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Docker installed for building the image.
